title: This Will Pass. I Am Safe.
subtitle: An affirmation for an acute anxiety episode
excerpt: A short affirmation for use during an acute anxiety episode or a moment of high physical distress. Two plain sentences that state what is factually true about the present moment.

practice.whenToUse: Use during an acute anxiety episode, a panic response, or a moment of high physical distress when the body is responding as if there is a threat.

content.text: A two-sentence affirmation for an acute anxiety episode. Both sentences are factual statements about the present moment. They are not aspirational. They are true right now.
Say it out loud, slowly, as many times as needed. If you can, place one hand on your chest while you say it.
This will pass. I am safe.

The first sentence is a statement about time: the episode is temporary. It has passed before. It will pass this time. The second sentence is a statement about the present: in this moment, the actual physical threat level is low, even if the body is responding as if it is not. Both are true. Both are useful to hear in a moment when the nervous system is not producing accurate information about danger.
